raw_consumer_statement:
The complaint is concerns Citi credit card company. On XX/XX/year>, I entered a rental car agreement with XXXX car rental in XXXX XXXX XXXX through XXXX who was their agent. The rental period was from XX/XX/year> through XX/XX/year>. The car was a XXXX XXXX. The total rental amount was {$160.00} which was confirmed by both XXXX and XXXX. The itinerary number was XXXX. When I arrived at the airport in XXXX XXXX, my wife and I were shuttled by XXXX to their rental facility a few miles away from the airport. When we arrived at the XXXX rental facility, the XXXX agent refused to honor the rental agreement and stated that we would have to pay {$500.00} in order to rent the car. There were no other car rental business in the area so we were forced to rental the car for that amount. We returned the car on XX/XX/XXXX with absolutely no damage which XXXX acknowledged. 

When my wife and I returned home, I filed a dispute with Citi requesting a refund in the amount of {$330.00}. Multiple times, I have submitted via email and certified XXXX mail, the rental agreement in the amount of {$160.00}, the receipt from XXXX in the amount of {$500.00}, and a sign affidavit stating the entire situation as proof of my dispute. I keep getting email letter from Citi 's dispute department claiming the they have either lost my submittals, never received them, or received them but they illegible. I have spoken countless times with Citi Customer Service representatives who state that they have received everything I have sent, it is perfectly legible, and I have done everything I need to do the settle this dispute. The dispute department, however, keep asking for the same thing over and over again. At this stage they are clearly stonewalling me and trying to wear me down. I also consider this harassment. I want a {$330.00} permanent credit back to my Citi credit card.
